zh.Stapleton, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾斯塔普萊頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sibyl Bellew於大約1270年出生在英國越可爾卡爾頓時，她的父親John de Bellew27歲，母親Laderina de Brus30歲。她於大約1286年與Sir Miles Stapleton I在英國越可爾貝達爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1301年10月1日在英國越可爾斯塔普萊頓享年32歲，埋葬在英國越可爾約克。

zh-hans.Stapleton, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾斯塔普萊頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sibyl Bellew于大约1270年出生在英国越可爾卡爾頓时，她的父亲John de Bellew27岁，母亲Laderina de Brus30岁。她于大约1286年与Sir Miles Stapleton I在英国越可爾貝達爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1301年10月1日在英国越可爾斯塔普萊頓享年32岁，埋葬在英国越可爾約克。

nameMeanings.name: Bellew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Irish, and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from a lost or unidentified estate in or near Normandy named with Old French bel + eau ‘beautiful water’, as is clear from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English surname spelling de Belewe and its Latin rendering as de Bella Aqua. The source of the surname is often stated to be Bellou (Orne), but this is improbable, as its etymological form Be(r)lou requires a different etymology than bella aqua. Nor is the surname from Belleau in Lincolnshire; this is post-medieval re-formation of the medieval placename Helgelo (Old Norse ‘Helgi's glade or meadow’).
Variant or Americanized form of   Ballou  , a surname of probable French origin. Compare   Ballew   1,   Balliew  ,   Belew   1,   Belue  , and   Billue  .
History: In Ireland this upper gentry surname (see 1 above) has been established since the 13th century in Duleek (Meath), where its estate has been known as Bellewstown since the 16th century.

variants.name: Belue
meanings: Variant of   Bellew   2, a surname of probable French origin (see   Ballou   1 and 2).

variants.name: Balliew
meanings: Altered form of   Bellew   2 and probably also a variant of   Ballew   1, surnames of probable French origin (see   Ballou   1 and 2).

variants.name: Belew
meanings: Variant of   Bellew   2, a surname of probable French origin (see   Ballou   1 and 2).
English, Irish, and Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Bellew   1. This form of the surname has apparently died out in Britain.

variants.name: Bellow
meanings: English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English beli, belu, or below ‘bellows’ for a bellows maker or blower, for example for a blacksmith or for a church organ. Until the early 15th century the term was normally used in the singular.

variants.name: Ballou
meanings: French: possibly an altered form of French and Flemish Ballieu or Balliu, surnames mainly found in Nord in France and in neighbouring Flanders in Belgium. If so, it is either habitational, from one of the numerous places in Picardy, Artois, and Normandy called Bailleul (Old French Balliol), or an occupational name from Old Picard and Middle Dutch bailli(e)u, ba(i)lleu, baillu (Old French baillif, balliu) ‘bailiff’. Alternatively, the name Ballou might be from the ancient Germanic personal name Balowulf, composed of the elements balo ‘torment, wickedness’ and wulf ‘wolf’. The surname Ballou is rare in France.

Alternatively, an altered form of French   Belleau   or perhaps   Beaulieu  . The name is sometimes (also) said to be an altered form of   Boileau   but this is problematic in terms of family history and phonological development. Compare   Ballew  ,   Ballow   1,   Belew  ,   Bellew  , and   Belue  .
Alternatively, a rare variant of English   Ballow   2 or an altered form of English   Bellew   1.

variants.name: Billue
meanings: Variant of   Bellew   2, a surname of probable French origin (see   Ballou   1 and 2).
Possibly also an altered form of English, Irish, and Scottish   Bellew   1.

variants.name: Belleau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel.

habitational name from Belleau, principally the name of a place in Meurthe-et-Moselle, which is recorded as early as 1047 as Bella Aqua (‘lovely water’) in Latin. It may also have been the name of unidentified or lost hamlets elsewhere in France (compare Bellew 1). Belleau in Aisne is a further possible source but this placename, recorded as Balliolum in 1231, has a different origin and it is not known if it gave rise to a surname or whether its re-formation as Belleau (a piece of folk etymology) took place early enough to affect the pronunciation of any such surname.

nameMeanings.name: Sibyl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Sybil .  Even in classical times there was confusion between the two vowels in this word.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sibyl Bellew was born about 1270, in Carlton, Yorkshire, England, her father, John de Bellew, was 27 and her mother, Laderina de Brus, was 30. She married Sir Miles Stapleton I about 1286, in Bedale, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1 October 1301, in Stapleton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 32, and was buried in York, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sibyl Bellew est née environ 1270, à Carlton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John de Bellew, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Laderina de Brus, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Miles Stapleton I environ 1286, à Bedale, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1 octobre 1301, à Stapleton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterrée à York, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sibyl Bellew um 1270 in Carlton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Bellew 27 und ihre Mutter Laderina de Brus 30. Sie hat um 1286 in Bedale, Yorkshire, England Sir Miles Stapleton I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1. Oktober 1301 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Stapleton, Yorkshire, England und wurde in York, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sibyl Bellew è nata il circa 1270 nel Carlton, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, John de Bellew, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Laderina de Brus, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir Miles Stapleton I circa 1286, nel Bedale, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1 ottobre 1301, nel Stapleton, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolta a York, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sibyl Bellewが約1270年にイギリスヨークシュアイアカールトンで生まれた時，父John de Bellewは27歳，母Laderina de Brusは30歳であった。約1286年にイギリスヨークシュアイアベダルでSir Miles Stapleton Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1301年10月1日にイギリスヨークシュアイアスタプレトンで32歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアヨークにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sibyl Bellew(이)가 대략 1270년 영국 요르크실르 칼턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Bellew(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Laderina de Brus(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 대략 1286년 영국 요르크실르 베데일에서 Sir Miles Stapleton I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1301년 10월 1일 영국 요르크실르 스타플레턴에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 요르크에 묻혔다.

mg.about 1270–about 1 October 1301: tany ho any amin'ny 1270–tany ho any amin'ny 1 ôktôbra 1301

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sibyl Bellew nasceu aproximadamente 1270, em Carlton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Bellew, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Laderina de Brus, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Miles Stapleton I aproximadamente 1286, em Bedale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1 de outubro de 1301, em Stapleton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 32 anos, e foi sepultada em York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sibyl Bellew родилась около 1270 г. в местности карлтон, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Bellew было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Laderina de Brus, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Miles Stapleton I около 1286 г. в местности бедеил, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1 октября 1301 г. в местности стаплетон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 32 лет и была похоронена в местности ерк, еркшир, Англия.

sm.about 1270–about 1 October 1301: e tusa o le 1270–e tusa o le 1 Oketopa 1301

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sibyl Bellew nació aproximadamente 1270, en Carlton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Bellew, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Laderina de Brus, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir Miles Stapleton I aproximadamente 1286, en Bedale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1 de octubre de 1301, en Stapleton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultada en York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
